question: hello
response: Hello! Welcome to the Constitution & Legal Help Assistant. I can help you with questions about Indian laws, your rights, and legal procedures. What would you like to know?

question: help
response:
I can help you with: 
• Your legal rights as a citizen
• Information about specific Indian laws
• How to file complaints (FIR, RTI, Consumer Forum)
• Understanding government schemes
• Legal procedures explained in simple language

Just type your query and I'll do my best to help!

question: what is constitution
response:
The Constitution of India, adopted on 26 November 1949, is the supreme law of India. It establishes the framework of government and guarantees Fundamental Rights to all citizens including:

• Right to Equality (Articles 14-18)
• Right to Freedom (Articles 19-22)
• Right against Exploitation (Articles 23-24)
• Right to Freedom of Religion (Articles 25-28)
• Cultural and Educational Rights (Articles 29-30)
• Right to Constitutional Remedies (Article 32)

The Constitution also has Directive Principles guiding state policy and Fundamental Duties of citizens.

question: how to file fir
response:
To file an FIR (First Information Report):

1. Visit the nearest police station with jurisdiction
2. You have the RIGHT to file an FIR — police cannot refuse
3. Give a written or oral complaint about the incident
4. The officer will record it in the FIR register
5. Demand a FREE copy of the FIR (it is your right under Section 154 CrPC)
6. Note the FIR number for future reference

If police refuse to file FIR:
• Approach the Superintendent of Police (SP)
• File a complaint with the Magistrate under Section 156(3) CrPC
• You can also file an e-FIR at your state police portal

Emergency: Call 100 (Police) or 112 (National Emergency)

question: how to file rti
response:
To file an RTI (Right to Information) Application:

1. Write an application (in English, Hindi, or local official language)
2. Clearly state the information you seek
3. Address it to the Public Information Officer (PIO) of the concerned department
4. Pay INR 10 as application fee (BPL families are exempt)
5. Submit in person, by post, or online

Online RTI filing: https://rtionline.gov.in

Timeline:
• PIO must respond within 30 days
• 48 hours if it concerns life and liberty

If unsatisfied: File First Appeal to Appellate Authority, then Second Appeal to Central/State Information Commission.

question: consumer complaint
response:
To file a Consumer Complaint:

1. First, contact the company/seller directly
2. If unresolved, file at National Consumer Helpline: 1800-11-4000 (toll-free)
3. File online at: https://consumerhelpline.gov.in

Consumer Courts (based on claim amount):
• District Commission: Up to INR 1 crore
• State Commission: INR 1 crore to 10 crore
• National Commission (NCDRC): Above INR 10 crore

Documents needed:
• Purchase receipt/invoice
• Communication with company
• Photos of defect if applicable

Filing fee is minimal and you can appear without a lawyer.

question: labour rights
response:
Key Labour Rights in India:

• Minimum Wages: Every worker is entitled to minimum wages fixed by state government
• Working Hours: Maximum 8-9 hours/day, 48 hours/week (Factories Act)
• Overtime: Paid at double the rate for hours beyond 48/week
• Provident Fund (EPF): Mandatory for establishments with 20+ employees
• ESIC: Health insurance for employees earning up to INR 21,000/month
• Maternity Leave: 26 weeks paid maternity leave
• Gratuity: After 5 years of service, payable at termination/retirement

For complaints: Contact the Labour Commissioner's office in your district or file online at https://labour.gov.in

question: domestic violence
response:
If you are facing domestic violence, here is what you should know:

Immediate Help:
• Call Women Helpline: 181
• Call Police: 100 or 112
• Contact National Commission for Women: 7827170170

Legal Remedies under Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005:
• Protection Order: Prevents abuser from contacting you
• Residence Order: Right to stay in shared household
• Monetary Relief: For expenses and losses
• Custody Order: For children

Steps:
1. File complaint at the nearest police station
2. Contact a Protection Officer at your local magistrate court
3. Apply to Magistrate for protection orders

One Stop Centres (Sakhi Centres): Provide shelter, police help, legal aid, and counseling — available in every district.

question: education rights
response:
Education Rights in India:

• Free and Compulsory Education: Children aged 6-14 have a Fundamental Right to free education (RTE Act, 2009)
• Private schools must reserve 25% seats for EWS (Economically Weaker Section) students
• Schools CANNOT charge capitation fees or conduct screening tests
• Mid-day meal must be provided in government schools

For RTE Admission:
1. Apply online through your state's RTE portal
2. Documents needed: Birth certificate, residence proof, income certificate
3. Admission must be given by age-appropriate class

For Scholarships: Visit https://scholarships.gov.in

Complaints: Contact District Education Officer (DEO) or file at https://pgportal.gov.in

question: income tax
response:
Income Tax Filing Guide:

Who must file ITR:
• Income above INR 3 lakh (new regime) or INR 2.5 lakh (old regime)
• If TDS has been deducted from salary
• For claiming tax refunds

Key Dates:
• ITR filing due: July 31 (individuals)
• Belated return: December 31 (with penalty)

Popular Deductions (Old Regime):
• 80C: Up to INR 1.5 lakh (LIC, PPF, ELSS, tuition fees)
• 80D: Health insurance premium
• HRA: House Rent Allowance exemption
• Standard Deduction: INR 50,000 for salaried employees

File online at: https://eportal.incometax.gov.in
Helpline: 1800-103-0025 (toll-free)

question: cyber crime complaint
response:
If you are a victim of a cyber crime:

Immediate Steps:
1. Do NOT share any more OTP/passwords
2. Block your card immediately (call bank helpline)
3. Save all screenshots, chats, transaction IDs as evidence

Report At:
• National Cyber Crime Portal: https://cybercrime.gov.in
• Helpline: 1930 (National Cyber Crime Helpline)
• Nearest Cyber Crime Police Station

For Financial Fraud:
• Report within 24 hours for best chance of recovery
• Banks are required to reverse fraudulent transactions reported promptly

Common Cybercrimes:
• UPI/Banking fraud: Report to bank + 1930
• Social media hack: Report to platform + cybercrime.gov.in
• Sextortion/Blackmail: Report to cyber cell immediately

question: passport application
response:
Passport Application Process:

1. Register at: https://passportindia.gov.in
2. Fill online application form (fresh/renewal/tatkal)
3. Pay fees online:
   • Normal: INR 1,500 (36 pages), INR 2,000 (60 pages)
   • Tatkal: Additional INR 2,000
4. Book appointment at nearest Passport Seva Kendra (PSK)
5. Visit PSK with original documents

Documents Required:
• Proof of Identity (Aadhaar/PAN/Voter ID)
• Proof of Address (Aadhaar/utility bill)
• Date of Birth proof (birth certificate/school leaving)
• Recent passport-size photos (white background)

Delivery:
• Normal: 30-45 days
• Tatkal: 7-14 days

Helpline: 1800-258-1800 (toll-free)

question: property dispute
response:
Property Dispute Resolution:

Common Types:
• Title disputes, Boundary disputes, Inheritance conflicts, Builder fraud

Steps to Resolve:
1. Gather all property documents (title deed, sale deed, mutation records)
2. Check property records at Sub-Registrar's office
3. File complaint at local police station if fraud/encroachment
4. Approach District Collector for land revenue matters
5. File civil suit in Civil Court

For Builder Fraud:
• File complaint with RERA (Real Estate Regulatory Authority)
• State RERA portal available for each state
• RERA must resolve within 60 days

For Mutation:
• Apply at Tehsil/Taluka office with required documents

Legal Aid: If you cannot afford a lawyer, contact State Legal Services Authority (free legal aid for eligible persons)

question: startup registration
response:
Starting a Business in India:

Choose Business Structure:
• Sole Proprietorship: Simplest, no separate registration, unlimited liability
• Partnership Firm: 2+ partners, register at Registrar of Firms
• LLP: Limited liability, register on MCA portal
• Private Limited Company: Separate legal entity, min. 2 directors and shareholders

Registration Steps (Private Limited / LLP):
1. Apply for DIN (Director Identification Number)
2. Reserve company name on MCA21 portal
3. File incorporation forms (SPICe+ for companies)
4. Get Certificate of Incorporation
5. Apply for PAN, TAN, GST, and open bank account

Cost: INR 7,000 - 15,000 approximately
Timeline: 7-15 working days

Startup India Recognition: https://startupindia.gov.in (get tax benefits)
MCA Portal: https://mca.gov.in

question: women helpline
response:
Women Safety & Helpline Numbers:

🆘 Emergency: 112
📞 Women Helpline: 181
📞 Domestic Violence: 181
📞 NCW Helpline: 7827170170
📞 Police: 100
📞 Child Helpline: 1098

For Workplace Harassment (POSH):
• Report to your company's Internal Complaints Committee (ICC)
• Every company with 10+ employees must have ICC
• If no ICC exists, approach Local Complaints Committee (LCC) at District level

For Stalking/Cyberstalking:
• File FIR at nearest police station
• Report at https://cybercrime.gov.in

Support Services:
• One Stop Centres (Sakhi Centres): Shelter + legal aid + counseling
• Swadhar Greh Scheme: Shelter homes for distressed women

NCW Website: https://ncw.nic.in

question: banking complaint
response:
Banking Complaint Resolution:

Step 1: Contact your bank's branch manager
Step 2: Write to bank's grievance redressal officer
Step 3: If unresolved in 30 days, approach:

🏦 Banking Ombudsman (FREE, no lawyer needed):
• Online: https://bankingombudsman.rbi.org.in
• All types of banking complaints covered
• RBI Helpline: 14448

For UPI/Digital Payment Fraud:
• Report immediately to bank: Block card/account
• Call Cyber Crime Helpline: 1930
• File online at: https://cybercrime.gov.in

For Cheque Bounce (Section 138 NI Act):
• Send legal notice within 30 days of bounce
• File criminal complaint in Magistrate Court if payment not made within 15 days of notice

Credit Information Dispute:
• Contact CIBIL/Experian/CRIF at their dispute portals
• File with RBI Ombudsman if bank refuses to correct

question: fundamental rights
response:
Fundamental Rights guaranteed by the Indian Constitution:

📜 Article 14: Right to Equality before law
📜 Article 15: Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex
📜 Article 19: Six freedoms including speech, assembly, movement, profession
📜 Article 20: Protection against arbitrary arrest and conviction
📜 Article 21: Right to Life and Personal Liberty (most important)
📜 Article 21A: Right to Education (6-14 years)
📜 Article 22: Protection from arbitrary arrest and detention
📜 Article 25-28: Freedom of Religion
📜 Article 32: Right to approach Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights

If your Fundamental Rights are violated:
• File a Writ Petition in High Court (Article 226) or Supreme Court (Article 32)
• Types of writs: Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, Certiorari, Prohibition, Quo Warranto
• Legal aid is available free of cost through NALSA: https://nalsa.gov.in

question: legal aid
response:
Free Legal Aid in India:

Who is eligible:
• Women and children
• SC/ST communities
• Persons with disabilities
• Victims of trafficking
• Industrial workmen
• Persons in custody
• Anyone with annual income below INR 3 lakh

How to get free legal aid:
1. Contact District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) at your district court
2. Apply online at: https://nalsa.gov.in
3. Call NALSA helpline: 15100

Services provided:
• Free legal advice
• Free representation by lawyer in court
• Mediation and settlement
• Legal literacy programs

Note: Legal aid lawyers are government-empaneled and must provide quality service. If unsatisfied, complain to DLSA.

question: retirement
response:
Retirement Benefits in India:

📋 Employees' Provident Fund (EPF):
• Can withdraw full amount after retirement (age 58)
• Partial withdrawal allowed for specific purposes (house, illness, marriage)
• Apply online at: https://epfindia.gov.in (Claim Form 19)

📋 Gratuity:
• Payable after 5 years of continuous service
• Formula: Last salary x 15/26 x years of service
• Maximum: INR 20 lakh
• Must be paid within 30 days of eligibility

📋 National Pension System (NPS):
• Available for government and private sector employees
• Tax benefit under Section 80CCD
• Managed by PFRDA: https://npscra.nsdl.co.in

📋 Senior Citizens:
• Higher tax exemption limit (INR 3 lakh for 60+, INR 5 lakh for 80+)
• Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) available
• Priority service at government offices and banks is your right

question: court procedure
response:
How to Approach Courts in India:

Types of Courts:
• Magistrate Court: Criminal cases, petty civil matters
• Civil Court / District Court: Civil disputes, family matters
• High Court: Appeals from lower courts, constitutional matters
• Supreme Court: Final appellate court, constitutional matters

For Civil Cases:
1. Draft a plaint (complaint) with facts and relief sought
2. File at appropriate civil court (based on jurisdiction and claim value)
3. Pay court fees (based on claim amount)
4. Serve summons to opposite party
5. Attend hearings

For Criminal Cases:
1. File FIR at police station
2. Or file private complaint directly before Magistrate

Fast Track Options:
• Lok Adalat: Quick, free, compromise-based settlement
• Consumer Forum: For consumer disputes
• Labour Court: For employment disputes
• Family Court: For matrimonial and custody matters

Lok Adalat dates: Check at your District Court or https://nalsa.gov.in

question: gst registration
response:
GST Registration Guide:

Who must register:
• Businesses with turnover above INR 40 lakh (goods) or INR 20 lakh (services)
• E-commerce sellers (regardless of turnover)
• Interstate supply businesses
• Specified categories (taxi aggregators, etc.)

Registration Process:
1. Visit https://gst.gov.in
2. Click New Registration
3. Fill details (PAN, business details, bank account)
4. Upload documents (PAN, Aadhaar, bank statement, business proof)
5. Verify with OTP
6. Receive GSTIN within 3-7 working days

Key Compliance:
• File GSTR-1 (monthly/quarterly): Outward supplies
• File GSTR-3B (monthly): Summary return
• Annual return: GSTR-9 by December 31

Composition Scheme (for turnover up to INR 1.5 crore): Lower flat rate tax, fewer compliances

GST Helpline: 1800-103-4786 (toll-free)

question: police rights
response:
Your Rights When Dealing With Police:

✅ Rights During Arrest:
• Police MUST inform you of the reason for arrest
• Right to know charges against you (Article 22)
• Right to be produced before Magistrate within 24 hours
• Right to inform family/friend of arrest
• Right to a lawyer (you can call your lawyer immediately)
• Right against self-incrimination (cannot be forced to confess)
• Female cannot be arrested after sunset and before sunrise except in exceptional circumstances

✅ Rights in Police Station:
• Right to legal aid (free lawyer if you cannot afford one)
• Right against torture (custodial torture is illegal and criminal)
• Right to medical examination if alleging police brutality

✅ If Police Violate Your Rights:
• File complaint with Superintendent of Police (SP)
• Approach Human Rights Commission (NHRC or State HRC)
• File Writ of Habeas Corpus in High Court for illegal detention
• NHRC Helpline: 14433

question: divorce
response:
Divorce Process in India:

Types of Divorce:
1. Mutual Consent Divorce: Both parties agree
   • File joint petition in Family Court
   • 6-month cooling off period (can be waived)
   • Faster and less expensive

2. Contested Divorce: One party files
   • Grounds: Cruelty, adultery, desertion (2+ years), mental disorder, conversion of religion
   • Takes longer (1-5 years typically)

Documents Needed:
• Marriage certificate, address proofs, photos, income proof
• Evidence of grounds (for contested divorce)

Maintenance/Alimony:
• Section 125 CrPC: Magistrate can order maintenance for spouse and children
• No minimum, decided by court based on income and needs

Child Custody:
• Court decides based on 'best interest of child'
• Both parents have right to visit unless court restricts

Family Courts: Available in all districts for faster family dispute resolution. You can also try Mediation first — it is free, confidential, and faster.

question: insurance
response:
Insurance Rights and Complaint Process:

Your Rights as a Policyholder:
• Right to receive policy document within 30 days
• 15-day 'free look' period to return the policy
• Claims must be settled within 30 days (life insurance)
• Health insurance claims: 30 days for reimbursement, immediate for cashless

For Claim Rejection:
1. File complaint with your insurance company's grievance cell
2. If unresolved in 15 days, approach:
   • Insurance Ombudsman (free, covers claims up to INR 50 lakh)
   • Online: https://ecoi.co.in
   • IRDAI Bima Bharosa portal

IRDAI Helpline: 155255 or 1800-4254-732 (toll-free)

Common Valid Reasons for Claim Settlement:
• Policy must be active at time of claim
• Disclose all medical conditions honestly at time of purchase
• File claim within stipulated time

For Motor Insurance Claims:
• Inform police for accident/theft
• Inform insurer within 24-48 hours
• Do not move damaged vehicle before survey

question: aadhaar
response:
Aadhaar - Everything You Need to Know:

Aadhaar Enrollment:
• Visit nearest Aadhaar Enrollment Centre (Post Office, banks, etc.)
• Documents: One proof of identity + one proof of address
• Biometric data (fingerprints, iris) collected
• Aadhaar sent by post in 90 days

Aadhaar Update/Correction:
• Online update at: https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in
• Update name, address, DOB, gender, mobile number
• Documents must be uploaded for corrections
• Visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra for biometric update

Aaadhaar Rights:
• Aadhaar is NOT mandatory for most services
• Cannot be made mandatory as sole identity document
• UIDAI must protect your biometric data

Report Misuse/Fraud:
• UIDAI Helpline: 1947 (toll-free)
• Email: help@uidai.gov.in
• Aadhaar Virtual ID: Use instead of actual Aadhaar number for privacy

Check Aadhaar Authentication History: https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in

question: what is rti
response:
RTI - Right to Information:

The Right to Information Act, 2005 gives every citizen of India the RIGHT to ask for information from any government body.

What you can ask:
• Status of your application/file
• Government schemes and their beneficiaries
• Details of public spending
• Copies of government documents
• Reasons for government decisions affecting you

What you CANNOT ask:
• Information relating to national security
• Cabinet notes
• Personal information of other individuals
• Information received in confidence from foreign government

How to Apply:
• Online: https://rtionline.gov.in
• Write to Public Information Officer (PIO) of the department
• Fee: INR 10 (postal order / demand draft / online)
• Response within 30 days (48 hours for life/liberty issues)

Remember: If PIO does not respond or gives incomplete information, you can appeal to First Appellate Authority and then to Central/State Information Commission.

question: voter id
response:
Voter ID and Voting Rights:

Eligibility:
• Indian citizen aged 18 years and above
• Resident of the constituency

New Voter Registration:
1. Visit: https://voters.eci.gov.in or National Voter Services Portal (NVSP)
2. Fill Form 6 (new registration)
3. Upload documents: Proof of age, proof of residence, photo
4. Verification done by BLO (Booth Level Officer)
5. Voter ID delivered or downloadable as e-EPIC

For Corrections/Updates:
• Form 8: Correction of entries
• Form 8A: Change of address within constituency

Voter Rights:
• Right to secret ballot
• Right to NOTA (None of the Above) option
• Cannot be prevented from voting if name is on electoral roll

Model Code of Conduct violations:
• Report to ECI: 1950 (National Voter Helpline) or cVIGIL app

Download e-EPIC: https://voters.eci.gov.in

question: air pollution
response:
How to Report Pollution:

For Air/Water/Noise Pollution:
1. Contact State Pollution Control Board (SPCB) of your state
2. File complaint online at: https://cpcb.nic.in
3. Approach National Green Tribunal (NGT) for serious cases

For Noise Pollution:
• Permitted levels: 55 dB (day), 45 dB (night) in residential areas
• File complaint at local police station
• Can also approach District Collector

For Industrial Pollution:
• Industries must have consent to operate from SPCB
• Report violations to SPCB with photos/videos as evidence
• NGT has given several landmark judgments for environmental protection

For Burning Crop/Waste:
• Report to local SPCB or municipal authority
• Crop burning is banned in NCR states

NGT Helpline: 011-23222705
CPCB: https://cpcb.nic.in

Under EPA 1986, penalties can be up to INR 1 lakh per day of violation.
